seoTitle: Mount Jackson vs Mount Lawley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Jackson and Mount Lawley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Jackson vs Mount Lawley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Jackson and Mount Lawley.
heading: Mount Jackson vs Mount Lawley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Jackson, Western Australia and Mount Lawley, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Lawley has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Mount Jackson (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Jackson or Mount Lawley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Jackson has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Mount Lawley scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Jackson scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Lawley scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Jackson or Mount Lawley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Lawley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
